note: Enumeration-discipline ledger for the compact/medium/expanded adaptive-layout model (docs/design/01-foundations.md §7). It records the ONE native anchor of that model that exists in the tree today -- WIN-CON-007's Material-guarded below-medium overlay predicate (Int_DeckOverlayMinWidth=600 consumed by ShouldDeckOverlayCanvas) -- by CROSS-REFERENCING the sidebar-panels contract that already locks it, never re-asserting its markers. Every other surface §7 names (toolbar overflow WIN-SHL-002, notebookbar WIN-NAV-004, status-bar pane dropping WIN-NAV-008, dialog near-full-width) carries an explicit target-no-native-anchor placeholder so none is silently unenumerated. A negative-space guard scans sfx2/source and framework/source/uielement for any OTHER width-driven breakpoint slot and fails closed if one appears without a matching ledger entry. This does NOT move WIN-FND-006's own SRC/MATRIX/FLOW/A11Y/LOC/PERF gates: the single populated anchor is owned and locked by WIN-CON-007, not this row. Source/text evidence only; runtime_verified:false; no build, resize, keyboard-order, or pixel evidence is claimed.

anchors.note: Cross-referenced from the sidebar-panels contract, not re-asserted here. Only suppresses force-widening the docked sidebar below the threshold; the pixel float-over-canvas presentation remains future work (build-bound).

anchors.surface: toolbar overflow / adaptive command layout (compact & medium classes)
anchors.note: §7 requires a designed overflow menu with stable keyboard order; no native width-driven overflow breakpoint exists in framework/source/uielement today.
Upstream notebookbar surface exists but has no Material width-driven adaptive breakpoint.

anchors.surface: status bar low-priority pane dropping (medium class)
anchors.note: §7 medium class drops low-priority status-bar panes; no native width predicate exists yet.

§7 compact class takes dialogs near-full-width (prototype caps at min(680px,94%)); no native width-driven dialog behaviour exists outside the prototype, so the cross-cutting foundation row holds this placeholder until a dialog row implements it.
